title: [InstCombine] Recursively replace condition with constant in select arms

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @cond_negated_in_select_arm(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
  %not_cond = xor i1 %cond, true
  %inner_sel = select i1 %cond, i1 %not_cond, i1 true
  %outer_sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b
  %result = select i1 %inner_sel, i32 %outer_sel, i32 0
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on direct condition substitution but doesn't explicitly mention handling negated conditions (xor/not) within select arms. The patch's replaceInInstruction handles this by substituting the condition constant, which then simplifies the negation operation.

alive2.src:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @cond_negated_in_select_arm(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
  %not_cond = xor i1 %cond, true
  %inner_sel = select i1 %cond, i1 %not_cond, i1 true
  %outer_sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b
  %result = select i1 %inner_sel, i32 %outer_sel, i32 0
  ret i32 %result
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @cond_negated_in_select_arm(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
  %result = select i1 %cond, i32 0, i32 %b
  ret i32 %result
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @cond_negated_in_select_arm(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
#0:
  %not_cond = xor i1 %cond, 1
  %inner_sel = select i1 %cond, i1 %not_cond, i1 1
  %outer_sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b
  %result = select i1 %inner_sel, i32 %outer_sel, i32 0
  ret i32 %result
}
=>
define i32 @cond_negated_in_select_arm(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
#0:
  %result = select i1 %cond, i32 0, i32 %b
  ret i32 %result
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i1 @cond_in_icmp_within_select_arm(i1 %cond, i32 %x) {
  %cmp = icmp eq i1 %cond, true
  %inner_sel = select i1 %cond, i1 %cmp, i1 false
  %outer_sel = select i1 %cond, i1 %inner_sel, i1 true
  ret i1 %outer_sel
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes condition substitution in select operands but doesn't explicitly cover cases where the condition is used as an operand to comparison instructions (icmp) within the select arms. The recursive replacement handles this by substituting into the icmp.

alive2.src:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i1 @cond_in_icmp_within_select_arm(i1 %cond, i32 %x) {
  %cmp = icmp eq i1 %cond, true
  %inner_sel = select i1 %cond, i1 %cmp, i1 false
  %outer_sel = select i1 %cond, i1 %inner_sel, i1 true
  ret i1 %outer_sel
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @cond_in_icmp_within_select_arm(i1 %cond, i32 %x) {
#0:
  %cmp = icmp eq i1 %cond, 1
  %inner_sel = select i1 %cond, i1 %cmp, i1 0
  %outer_sel = select i1 %cond, i1 %inner_sel, i1 1
  ret i1 %outer_sel
}
=>
define i1 @cond_in_icmp_within_select_arm(i1 %cond, i32 %x) {
#0:
  ret i1 1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @cond_zext_in_select_arm(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
  %ext = zext i1 %cond to i32
  %inner_sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %ext, i32 0
  %outer_sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %inner_sel, i32 %b
  ret i32 %outer_sel
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary doesn't mention type conversion operations (zext, trunc, bitcast) on the condition within select arms. The patch's generic replaceInInstruction handles these by substituting the condition constant before the type conversion, enabling constant folding.

alive2.src:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @cond_zext_in_select_arm(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
  %ext = zext i1 %cond to i32
  %inner_sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %ext, i32 0
  %outer_sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %inner_sel, i32 %b
  ret i32 %outer_sel
}

  %outer_sel = select i1 %cond, i32 1, i32 %b
  ret i32 %outer_sel
}
